CT104670: Our best-selling Carhartt jacket by a wide margin — more than 500 Storm Defender Shorelines embroidered across a hundred-plus company orders. Storm Defender is Carhartt's waterproof-breathable shell, which makes this the jacket Northwest crews actually wear in January rain instead of leaving in the truck. The chest takes a clean embroidered logo, and companies reorder it season after season.
CT104050: The Washed Duck Active Jac is the classic Carhartt silhouette in pre-washed duck canvas, so it feels broken-in on day one instead of after a season. It's one of our most-embroidered winter jackets — over 300 have gone out with company logos — and the hood-and-blanket-lined build handles a Northwest job site from October through March.
CTSJ140: The quilted-flannel-lined version of Carhartt's Duck Active Jac adds a warmer core for crews that work through real cold. We've logoed batches for excavation and concrete companies who wanted the classic duck shell with more insulation than the thermal-lined standby. Left-chest embroidery on duck canvas is about as durable as decoration gets.
CT105534: Super Dux is Carhartt's modern technical line, and this soft shell is its flagship — a cleaner, more athletic cut than traditional duck, with stretch and weather resistance built in. Companies pick it when they want the Carhartt label on something a project manager will wear to a client meeting. It embroiders beautifully on the chest.
CT102199: The Crowley is Carhartt's take on the company soft shell — wind-blocking, water-shedding, and cut clean enough for the office half of the job. It's a staple of our uniform programs for field-service companies: one jacket that works in the truck, on the site, and at the counter, with an embroidered logo that reads professional everywhere.
CT104277: The Sherpa-Lined Mock Neck Vest turned into one of our most-reordered Carhartt pieces — 80-plus separate orders — because crews layer it over hoodies three seasons a year. The sherpa core is genuinely warm, the mock neck blocks the wind, and the smooth chest panel is a natural spot for an embroidered company mark.
CT106678: The current generation of Carhartt's thermal-lined Duck Active Jac — the hooded work jacket that's been on Northwest job sites for decades, updated fit and all. The thermal lining suits crews that run warm while they work; the duck shell shrugs off abuse. We embroider it constantly for construction and site-work companies.
CT105535: The vest cut of Carhartt's Super Dux soft shell: core warmth and weather resistance with free arms for real work. It's the modern alternative to the classic duck vest — lighter, stretchier, more office-friendly — and the flat chest panel takes an embroidered logo cleanly.
CT105475: The Montana Insulated Vest has one of the most striking order patterns we track: 80-plus units across 80-plus orders — companies buy it one and two at a time as employee gifts and manager pieces. Real insulation, a modern Carhartt look, and a chest that embroiders cleanly. If you're building a holiday gift order, start here.
CT106677: Carhartt's current quilted-flannel-lined Duck Active Jac — the warmer sibling of the thermal-lined standard, for crews that work through genuine cold. Same legendary duck shell, same hooded silhouette, more insulation in the body. An embroidered left-chest logo on this jacket outlasts most of the trucks it rides in.
CTV01: The original Carhartt Duck Vest — the layering piece crews have worn for generations, in the same sandstone duck as the jackets. It goes over a hoodie in fall and under a shell in winter, and the chest takes a clean embroidered mark. A company order of these never sits in the closet.
CT106676: Carhartt's current-generation Duck Vest — the classic silhouette with updated fit and lining. Thirty-plus separate orders have come through our shop, mostly companies adding a vest option to an existing Carhartt program. Embroiders cleanly over the chest, layers over everything.
CT104314: The Women's Gilliam Jacket brings Carhartt's most popular lightweight-insulated line to a women's cut — same quilted warmth, tailored fit instead of a shrunken men's box. We add it to company orders so mixed crews get one jacket program that actually fits everyone; the embroidery matches the unisex version exactly.
CTJ131: The long-running classic: Carhartt's thermal-lined Duck Active Jac as generations of crews have known it. Hooded, tough, warm enough for most Northwest winters, and the single most-recognized work jacket silhouette in America. We embroider company logos on the chest and names on the front — the way shop jackets have always been done.
CT104315: The women's cut of the Gilliam Vest — Carhartt's lightweight quilted layering piece, tailored for women instead of merely sized down. Companies add it alongside the men's CT102286 so a whole-crew vest order fits the whole crew. Embroiders identically to its unisex twin.
CTT103828: The tall cut of the Duck Detroit Jacket — same blanket-lined duck canvas icon, with body and sleeve length for taller frames. We order it alongside the regular CT103828 so nobody on the crew ends up with cold wrists. Same embroidery, same look, correct proportions.
CTC003: The Duck Traditional Coat is the longest silhouette in Carhartt's duck lineup — extra coverage below the waist for crews working in wind and rain all day. It's the coat we recommend for equipment operators and inspectors who are outside but not always moving. Takes a big, clean embroidered back logo as well as the standard left chest.
CT104053: The Women's Washed Duck Active Jac gives the classic pre-washed duck work jacket a genuine women's fit — same broken-in feel, same hood, same toughness. When a company runs a duck-jacket program, we pair this with the CT104050 so every crew member gets the right cut.
CT106543: The Gilliam Insulated Hooded Jacket adds a hood and a warmer fill to Carhartt's popular lightweight quilted line — the step between the everyday Gilliam and a full winter coat. It layers clean, packs light, and takes chest embroidery like it was designed around it.
CTT104050: The tall version of Carhartt's Washed Duck Active Jac — full body and sleeve length so the broken-in duck classic fits crews over 6'2" the way it should. Ordered alongside the regular cut, decorated identically, nobody left with a jacket that rides up.
CT107082: The Montana Insulated Hooded Jacket is the full-jacket sibling of our most-gifted Carhartt vest — serious insulation, a modern cut, and a hood for the weather that finds every job site eventually. New to our lineup and built for whole-crew winter orders with a left-chest logo.
CT104293: The Sherpa-Lined Coat wraps Carhartt duck around a genuinely warm sherpa interior — the coat crews reach for when the thermal-lined jackets aren't enough. The duck shell takes embroidery cleanly, and the sherpa collar frames it like a catalog photo.
CT106439: The Rain Defender Soft Shell Vest carries Carhartt's rain-repellent finish in a layering cut — core protection from Northwest drizzle with arms free for work. A dozen separate orders have come through for it already; it pairs naturally with the matching soft shell jacket in a company program.
CT105533: The Super Dux Insulated Hooded Coat is the warmest piece in Carhartt's modern technical line — insulated, weather-resistant, and cut longer for coverage. It's the cold-storage and winter-site pick for companies that want current-generation Carhartt rather than the traditional duck look.
CT105532: The Rugged Flex Fleece-Lined Shirt Jac lives in the gap between flannel and jacket — Carhartt's stretch fabric outside, warm fleece inside, buttons up like a shirt and works like outerwear. Crews wear it all day indoors and out; an embroidered chest logo makes it uniform.
CT106416: The Textured Full-Zip Fleece Jacket is Carhartt's everyday fleece — the piece that replaces a generic corporate fleece with one carrying a label crews respect. It embroiders cleanly on the chest and works as both a mid-layer and a standalone shop jacket.
CT102524: The Women's Rugged Flex Crawford Jacket is Carhartt's stretch-duck work jacket in a true women's cut — movement built into the fabric, not just sized down. It's the pick for crews who found traditional duck too stiff, and it takes a left-chest logo cleanly.
CT105456: The Women's Montana Insulated Hooded Coat brings serious winter insulation to a women's cut — the cold-weather counterpart to the Montana vest and jacket line. Companies order it to complete a winter program so the whole crew, every fit, gets genuinely warm gear with a matching logo.
CTT104293: The tall cut of the Sherpa-Lined Coat — the same warm duck-and-sherpa build with the body and sleeve length taller crew members actually need. Ordered alongside the regular CT104293; embroidery matches exactly.
CTTC003: The tall version of the Duck Traditional Coat — Carhartt's longest work coat, proportioned for tall frames so the extra coverage lands where it should. Same duck toughness, same embroidery options, right fit.
CT107115: The Women's Rain Defender Soft Shell Jacket carries Carhartt's rain-repellent soft shell into a women's cut — the everyday Northwest jacket, tailored. Companies pair it with the men's version for a uniform program that looks intentional instead of improvised.
CTT106677: The tall cut of Carhartt's current quilted-flannel-lined Duck Active Jac — full-length warmth for tall crew members working through cold months. Same shell, same lining, same embroidery as the standard cut.
CTT106678: The tall version of the current thermal-lined Duck Active Jac — sleeves and body cut for tall frames so the classic hooded work jacket fits the whole crew. Decorated identically to the regular cut in the same run.
CTTSJ140: The tall cut of the quilted-flannel-lined Duck Active Jac — the warmer duck classic with tall proportions. We add it to crew orders so the tallest guys get the same jacket, not a compromise.
CT106438: Carhartt's current Rain Defender Soft Shell Jacket — wind-blocking, drizzle-shedding, and cut for movement, with the label that means something on a job site. It's the everyday Northwest company jacket; the chest panel takes embroidery cleanly.
CT106674: The current generation of Carhartt's Duck Traditional Coat — the longest, most protective silhouette in the duck line, updated. Extra coverage for operators and crews stationary in weather; big back-logo real estate plus the standard embroidered chest.
CTT106674: The tall cut of the current Duck Traditional Coat — full coverage, proportioned for tall frames. Same duck canvas, same decoration options as the standard cut.
CTTJ131: The tall version of the classic thermal-lined Duck Active Jac — the iconic hooded work jacket with real tall proportions. Ordered alongside CTJ131 so the whole crew matches, wrists covered.
CT106417: The 1/2-zip cut of Carhartt's Textured Fleece — pullover convenience, collar-up warmth, and the Carhartt label on an everyday mid-layer. Embroiders cleanly on the left chest; a natural step-up piece for company fleece programs.
CT106418: The Textured Fleece Vest is Carhartt's core-warmth layering piece in modern fleece — lighter and softer than the duck vests, easy over a work shirt or under a shell. A clean embroidery canvas for companies building out a layered uniform program.
CT106419: The Women's Textured Full-Zip Fleece Jacket gives Carhartt's everyday fleece a real women's cut. Companies add it so a fleece program fits the entire team properly — same fabric, same embroidered logo, tailored fit.
CT106171: The Carhartt ANSI Class 2 Mesh Zip-Front Vest puts real certified visibility under the Carhartt name — mesh construction for hot months, reflective taping, and compliance for DOT and site-spec work. We logo it by print or transfer without touching the certification.
CT106693: The ANSI 107 Class 3 Storm Defender Jacket is Carhartt's serious hi-vis: full Class 3 coverage plus a genuinely waterproof shell. It's for road, rail, and utility crews working dark Northwest winters — certified visibility that doesn't quit when the rain starts. Logos go on without affecting the rating.
CT104987: The Carhartt ANSI 107 Class 3 Hooded Sweatshirt does something crews love — it makes the required hi-vis layer feel like a real hoodie instead of a plastic shell. Certified Class 3 visibility, hood and all. We add company logos while keeping every inch of tape and rating intact.
CT104988: The lined full-zip version of Carhartt's Class 3 hi-vis sweatshirt — warmer, easier on and off over layers, same ANSI 107 certification. For crews that need visibility and warmth in one garment through the dark months.
CT106694: Carhartt's heaviest hi-vis piece: ANSI 107 Class 3 certified, waterproof, and insulated for full winter conditions. It's the jacket for utility and roadside crews who are out regardless of weather. Decoration is planned around the taping so certification stays intact.
CTK122: The zip-front version of Carhartt's midweight hoodie — nearly 600 through our shop across 140-plus orders. Foremen and drivers favor the zip for on-and-off over layers; crews order it alongside the pullover CTK121 in the same color with the same logo. Loose Carhartt fit, real warmth, embroidery that reads sharp on the chest.
CT100615: The pullover version of Carhartt's Rain Defender Paxton hoodie — heavyweight fleece with a rain-repellent finish, which in the Northwest is the difference between a hoodie and THE hoodie. Nearly 300 separate orders have come through for it, making it one of the most-reordered garments in our shop. Chest embroidery or full-back prints both work beautifully.
CT105294: The Midweight 1/4-Zip Mock Neck Sweatshirt is Carhartt's answer to the office quarter-zip — work-grade fleece with a collar that looks right at the counter and warm enough for the yard. It's become a quiet favorite in our uniform programs; 250-plus have gone out with logos.
CT102788: The Women's Clarksburg Full-Zip Hoodie is our most-ordered women's Carhartt piece — over 100 separate orders. It's a real women's cut of the heavyweight zip hoodie crews already wear, so mixed teams finally match. Soft inside, tough outside, and the chest takes an embroidered logo cleanly.
CT100614: The full-zip cut of Carhartt's Rain Defender Paxton heavyweight hoodie — the same rain-shedding fleece as the top-selling mock-zip and pullover, in an easy-layering full zip. Companies mix all three in one order, one logo, and let the crew pick their style.
CTK288: The Midweight Hooded Logo Sweatshirt comes with Carhartt's own graphic built in, and 90-plus separate orders tell us crews want exactly that — brand logo out front, company logo embroidered on the chest or sleeve alongside it. It's the hoodie for teams that want the label loud.
CTK124: The Midweight Crewneck is the hoodless workhorse of Carhartt's fleece line — warm, layerable under jackets and hard hats without hood bulk. A hundred-plus have gone through our embroidery heads; shops and warehouse crews are the usual buyers.
CTTK121: The tall cut of the CTK121 — our single highest-grossing Carhartt style — with real body and sleeve length for tall frames. Fifty-plus have shipped alongside regular-cut orders so the whole crew wears the same hoodie, properly fitted, identically embroidered.
CT104078: The Midweight Thermal-Lined Full-Zip Sweatshirt layers a thermal interior into Carhartt's standard zip hoodie — warmer than it looks, without jacket bulk. It's the piece for crews who wear a hoodie as their outer layer most of the year.
